data.content:
Here's my analysis of the build infrastructure. I found several significant issues:

---

## Issues Found

### 1. No `Directory.Build.props` — Properties duplicated in every project

All 5 projects repeat these identical settings:
```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

### 2. No Central Package Management — Version drift already present

| Package | Core | Api | Services |
|---------|------|-----|----------|
| `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` | 8.0.0 | 8.0.0 | **8.0.1** |
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | — | — | — |

| Package | Core.Tests | Api.Tests |
|---------|-----------|-----------|
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | **17.10.0** | **17.9.0** |
| `xunit` | **2.8.1** | **2.7.1** |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | **2.8.1** | **2.5.6** |

### 3. Duplicated custom target (`PrintBuildInfo`) in 3 src projects

### 4. Duplicated `DocumentationFile` property in 3 src projects

### 5. No `global.json` to pin SDK version

---

## Recommended Solution

Create 3 new files to centralize everything:

**`Directory.Build.props`** (repo root) — shared properties for all projects:
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- src projects generate XML docs -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Test projects are not packable -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**`Directory.Build.targets`** (repo root) — shared targets:
```xml
<Project>
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build"
          Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

**`Directory.Packages.props`** (repo root) — Central Package Management:
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Shared -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <!-- Test -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

After centralization, the individual `.csproj` files become minimal. For example, **`ContosoApp.Core.csproj`** shrinks to:
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

And **`ContosoApp.Core.Tests.csproj`**:
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" />
    <PackageReference Include="NSubstitute" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

---

## Summary of Benefits

| Before | After |
|--------|-------|
| 8 properties × 5 files = 40 lines of duplication | 1 central `Directory.Build.props` |
| Version drift across test/src projects | Single source of truth in `Directory.Packages.props` |
| Custom target copy-pasted 3× | 1 `Directory.Build.targets` |
| Adding a new project requires copying boilerplate | New projects inherit everything automatically |

Would you like me to implement these changes?
data.reasoningText:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me analyze the issues I see:

1. **Massive duplication across all project files:**
   - `TargetFramework`, `LangVersion`, `Nullable`, `ImplicitUsings`, `TreatWarningsAsErrors`, `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild`, `Company`, `Authors` are repeated in every single project file.
   
2. **No `Directory.Build.props` or `Directory.Build.targets`** — these are the standard MSBuild mechanism to centralize shared properties.

3. **No `Directory.Packages.props`** (Central Package Management) — package versions are scattered across project files with version drift:
   - `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` varies between `8.0.0` and `8.0.1` across projects
   - Test projects have inconsistent versions of xunit, xunit.runner.visualstudio, and Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk

4. **Duplicated custom target** (`PrintBuildInfo`) in all 3 src projects — should be centralized in a `.targets` file.

5. **Duplicated `DocumentationFile` property** across all src projects, and test infrastructure packages like xunit and NSubstitute are independently defined in each test project rather than shared. There's also no `global.json` to lock the SDK version.
